articles.title: Premium Watchlist Recap: New Zealand Inflation Reports (Q1 2026)
articles.expand: New Zealand’s Q1 CPI beat forecasts and repriced RBNZ expectations – here’s how our NZD watchlist setups held up.

articles.title: Fed: Policy on hold as Iran shock lingers – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir expect the Federal Reserve (Fed) to keep the Fed funds rate at 3.50–3.75% at the April Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC), with Chair Powell maintaining a neutral stance on future policy.

articles.title: WTI holds firm as stalled US-Iran talks and Hormuz disruptions support prices
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) Crude Oil holds steady on Monday, as stalled US-Iran peace talks reduce hopes that the Strait of Hormuz will reopen anytime soon. At the time of writing, WTI is trading around $95.00 per barrel, with shifting geopolitical headlines keeping volatility elevated.

articles.title: BoJ: Cautious stance and inflation guidance in focus – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley notes that earlier market surveys showed a strong chance of a Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate hike this week, but Governor Ueda’s lack of clear hints at IMF/World Bank meetings shifted expectations to June.

articles.title: USD/JPY retreats as Yen firms ahead of BoJ decision
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair is trading near the 159.30 price zone as markets are increasingly positioning for a hawkish hold from the BoJ, with policymakers expected to keep the benchmark rate unchanged at 0.75% while signaling a willingness to tighten further.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Cautiously bullish above 1.1700 ahead of FOMC and ECB
articles.expand: Technical analysis of EUR/USD: The pair has shifted to a cautiously bullish bias, supported by holding above the 1.1700 pivot zone. We detail chart setups, tactical bullish and bearish scenarios, and key levels to watch ahead of high-volatility events, specifically the FOMC and ECB meetings this week.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD edges up as stalled Iran talks keep markets on edge
articles.expand: GBP/USD registers modest gains during the North American session on Monday, up by 0.19%, as US-Iran talks stalled, while market mood remains fragile, as reflected in US equity markets trading lower. The pair trades at 1.3548, after bouncing off daily lows of 1.3506. Read More...

articles.title: Gold struggles despite softer USD as higher-for-longer interest rate bets weigh
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) trades with a mild downside bias on Monday despite a weaker US Dollar (USD), as macro headwinds stemming from the ongoing US-Iran war keep gains in check. At the time of writing, XAU/USD is trading around $4,669, down about 0.84% on the day after hitting an intraday peak of $4,730.

articles.title: EUR/GBP dips as Pound shrugs off UK political noise, ECB and BoE meetings in focus
articles.expand: EUR/GBP edges lower on Monday after brief volatility triggered by political jitters in the United Kingdom (UK). At the time of writing, the cross is trading around 0.8658, easing from an intraday high of 0.8676.

articles.title: USD/JPY: BoJ caution and intervention risk shape outlook – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s FX team highlights that Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate-hike expectations for April have collapsed, even as Japanese inflation data surprise to the upside.

articles.title: BoC: Policy hold view after turnover – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank strategists Molly Schwartz and Christian Lawrence expect the Bank of Canada (BoC) to keep its overnight policy rate at 2.25% through year-end, with no change anticipated at the April 29 meeting.

articles.title: Iran says it's looking into the US request for negotiations
articles.expand: On Telegram, Iran's foreign minister said the US has reached out to request negotiations and that they're looking into the request. On the weekend, Trump said Iran could call the USA if it wanted talks, now Iran says the opposite is underway. Who knows what the truth is but Trump is holding a meeting today in the Situation Room and that's the main ...

articles.title: ECB: June hike risk grows on energy shock – Standard Chartered
articles.expand: Standard Chartered strategists Christopher Graham and John Davies expect the European Central Bank (ECB) to keep the deposit rate at 2.00% on 30 April, citing a wait‑for‑data stance as the Middle East conflict evolves.

articles.title: Dollar Weakens and Gold Falls on New Iran Proposal to End War
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) today is down by -0.19%. The dollar fell from a 2.5-week high today and turned lower after Axios reported that Iran has offered a new proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The dollar initially moved higher in overnight trade after President Trump canceled planned...

articles.title: Oil: Hormuz risk and higher price path – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Bob Savage reports that Iran has proposed a U.S. deal prioritizing reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, with nuclear talks delayed.

articles.title: EUR/GBP: Loses momentum as BoE turns hawkish – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG analysts note that EUR/GBP has drifted lower within a 0.8600–0.8800 range as the Pound (GBP) outperforms the Euro (EUR). The euro-zone faces weaker PMIs and rising stagflation risks, while stronger United Kingdom (UK) data and sticky inflation have markets pricing more BoE tightening.

articles.title: USD: Data-heavy week shapes macro narrative – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir outline a busy United States (US) data calendar that will guide US Dollar (USD) traders, including Gross Domestic Product (GDP), Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index inflation, ISM manufacturing and consumer confidence.

articles.title: Fed: One more cut still expected – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank strategists reiterate its view that the Federal Reserve (Fed) is likely to cut rates again this year, even as United States (US) Treasury yields edge higher on persistent inflation concerns.

articles.title: Gold Declines Amid Geopolitics, with Optimism Limited
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex Gold fell below 4,700 USD per troy ounce on Monday, extending last week’s losses. Pressure on the precious metal has intensified following the breakdown of attempts to resume negotiations between the US and Iran, as well as the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz, supporting inflationary risks. articles.expand: The Globe Mail earlier reported that Canada would be setting up a sovereign wealth fund to back major projects and now we're getting some details. It will be endowed with $25 billion initially, which is a decent chunk of borrowing for a country Canada's size, and could be notable for the bond market, depending on how the money is raised. articles.title: EUR/USD: Energy shock and ECB delay rate response – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG strategists say EUR/USD has retreated toward the middle of its 1.1400–1.2000 range as the Euro faces headwinds from the Middle East energy shock and weaker euro-zone PMIs.

articles.title: USD/JPY: Breakout risk on BoJ surprise – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Chris Turner highlights unusually low USD/JPY volatility despite multiple global risks and key central bank meetings. Their economists warn markets underprice the risk of a Bank of Japan (BoJ) rate hike and possible upward revisions to inflation forecasts.

articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Bulls aim for 0.5930 amid US Dollar weakness
articles.expand: The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) is rallying against a weaker US Dollar (USD) for the second consecutive day on Monday, reaching session highs above 0.5900 at the time of writing, after bouncing at lows around 0.5840 on Friday.

articles.title: Major central banks are up against a very tough task in navigating monetary policy next
articles.expand: The central bank bonanza returns to town this week and will feature plenty of big names on the agenda. The BOJ, BOC, Fed, BOE, and ECB will be stepping up to the plate but are all expected to keep interest rates unchanged. The hot topic is the Middle East war and we are now officially nine weeks into that. And yet with all the talk of progress, the...

articles.title: Short-term inflation expectations rise, lending tightens in the latest ECB's SAFE survey
articles.expand: Full report here According to the ECB's latest Survey on the Access to Finance of Enterprises (SAFE), Eurozone companies faced significantly tighter bank lending conditions and a sharp rise in short-term inflation expectations during the first quarter of 2026. articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bears retain control amid Iran jitters, ahead of BoC/Fed
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair is seen extending its retracement from the 1.3715 resistance, or over a one-week set on Friday, and drifting lower for the second consecutive day.

articles.title: ECB to hold the line at this week's meeting - Goldman Sachs
articles.expand: ECB policymakers have made it quite clear that they are valuing optionality as we approach the next policy decision this week. And markets are also listening, paring back on rate hike pricing with odds of such a move now seen at ~20% only. Those odds do jump back up to ~63% by the time we get to the June meeting though. articles.title: BoJ preview: no change expected amid the US-Iran uncertainty
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan is widely expected to keep the policy rate steady at 0.75% tomorrow. The central bank will also release the quarterly outlook report where inflation is expected to be revised higher, while growth lower due to the US-Iran war. There were some speculations a couple of weeks ago that the BoJ could have hiked already in April, but the...

articles.title: Bank of Japan interest rate decision on Tuesday: Can BoJ hike short-term rates? How can it impact Indian stock market?
articles.expand: As the Bank of Japan prepares to announce its interest rate decision, the potential implications of the BoJ's policies amidst global tensions, particularly in West Asia, create uncertainty for investors.

articles.title: EUR/USD Forex Signal: Volatility Expected Ahead of Fed and ECB Decisions - 27 April 2026
articles.expand: The EUR/USD exchange rate held steady at 1.1700 on Monday morning ahead of a busy week in which the US and Europe will release their numbers and the European Central Bank (ECB) and the Federal Reserve will deliver their interest rate decisions.

articles.title: Chicago corn, wheat, soy rise on crude oil gains as US-Iran talks stall
articles.expand: BEIJING: Chicago Board of Trade corn , wheat , and soybean futures rose on Monday, tracking gains in the crude oil market, as US-Iran peace talks stalled over the weekend, leaving Gulf shipping blocked.‐Reuters

articles.title: Australia's Two-Speed Housing Market Cools as Sydney, Melbourne Prices Slip in 2026
articles.expand: SYDNEY — Australia's $12.6 trillion residential property market is showing clear signs of divergence and moderation in early 2026, with house prices falling in Sydney and Melbourne while resource-driven cities like Perth and Brisbane continue to post strong gains amid persistent supply shortages and shifting interest rate pressures.

articles.title: Revolutionary plan to get Boomers out of their big homes
articles.expand: A new plan could help free up some of Australia’s 13 million empty bedrooms as the nation’s housing shortage deepens.

articles.title: Bank of England Set to Keep Rates at 3.75% Amid Iran War; Inflation Risks Spark Rate Hike Bets
articles.expand: The Bank of England is expected to hold interest rates at 3.75% this Thursday as it assesses the economic damage from the Iran war. While Governor Andrew Bailey warns against premature moves, markets are pricing in hikes for July as UK inflation remains the highest in the G7.

articles.title: Germany May GfK consumer sentiment -33.3 vs -29.3 expected
articles.expand: Prior -28.0; revised to -28.1 The German consumer climate worsens to a three-year low as the fallout from the Middle East conflict continues to take a toll on Europe's largest economy. In particular, higher energy prices and in turn higher inflation is translating into a major worry for German households. And that is reflected by a nosedive in inco...

articles.title: Chart alert: AUD/USD kickstarts fresh bullish impulsive sequence above 0.7090 key support
articles.expand: AUD/USD is showing renewed bullish momentum after rebounding above 0.7090, supported by improving risk sentiment and easing geopolitical tensions around the Strait of Hormuz. The pair continues to track global equities closely, with correlation at elevated levels. A break above 0.7211 could trigger a fresh rally toward 0.7300, while failure to hold...

articles.title: Scotiabank Comments on Newmont’s FY2026 Earnings (NYSE:NEM)
articles.expand: Newmont Corporation (NYSE:NEM – Free Report) – Investment analysts at Scotiabank reduced their FY2026 earnings per share estimates for Newmont in a report released on Thursday, April 23rd. articles.expand: Is the Bank of Japan (BOJ) getting ready to adjust monetary policy this time or will they just step up their jawboning game? Here’s what you need to know when trading this central bank event.

articles.title: Heads up for Japan market holiday this week, and then three the following week
articles.expand: Tomorrow we get the Bank of Japan: Preview: BOJ expected to stay on hold next week but deliver hawkish signal on June move BOJ may lean more hawkishly to ease pressure on the yen - Nomura Holidays follow on Wednesday then Monday, Tuesday and Wednesday next week (Golden Week). This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Markets Weekly Outlook - Can earnings outweigh geopolitical headwinds & Central Bank decisions?
articles.expand: Global markets face a pivotal week defined by surging oil prices amid US-Iran tensions, record-high US equities driven by tech and earnings optimism, and critical central bank decisions. The Fed, ECB, and BoE grapple with the "balancing problem" of inflation vs. growth, while the BoJ remains the 'wild card' for a surprise rate hike.

articles.title: The USD is lower to kickstart Friday'trade. Iran sends delegation to Pakistan
articles.expand: The USD is softer heading into Friday trade, with the greenback slipping to fresh session lows as North American desks come in. Risk sentiment is getting a modest boost from headlines that Iran will send a delegation to Pakistan this weekend, helping lift equities after yesterday’s dip. On the corporate side, Intel is the standout. Shares are sharp...

articles.title: Volatile etf flows signal investor caution in the gold market
articles.expand: Gold markets experienced strong volatility in Q1, with record highs followed by sharp corrections. ETF flows reflected investor caution, shifting from strong inflows to outflows by March. High prices are dampening jewelry demand, while central bank actions add complexity. Despite weaker physical demand, geopolitical risks and expectations of looser...

articles.expand: SNB Chair Martin Schlegel warned that the Middle East conflict is injecting significant uncertainty into the global economy, with rising energy prices set to push inflation higher in the months ahead.
